assertions.expectation: Explains that spot VMs can be preempted at any time, which interacts with the upgrade drain process
assertions.reason: Response clearly states 'Spot VMs can be preempted during the upgrade process' and discusses preemption risk throughout

assertions.expectation: Notes that spot node pools may already have workloads designed for interruption (good for upgrades)
assertions.reason: Response does not mention that spot workloads are typically designed for interruption, which would make upgrades easier

assertions.expectation: Recommends higher maxSurge for spot pools since the workloads should already be fault-tolerant
assertions.reason: Response recommends conservative surge settings (maxSurge: 1) rather than higher maxSurge for spot pools

assertions.expectation: Advises setting maxUnavailable >0 for spot pools since workloads are already preemption-tolerant
assertions.reason: Response recommends maxUnavailable: 0, which is the opposite of the expected advice

assertions.expectation: Mentions that spot VM availability may affect surge node creation (not guaranteed to get spot instances)
assertions.reason: Response does not discuss potential issues with spot VM availability affecting the ability to create surge nodes

assertions.expectation: Recommends having PDBs even for spot workloads to ensure orderly drain during upgrade
assertions.reason: Response includes PDB verification commands and provides a PDB YAML example for workload protection

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command for configuring surge settings on the spot pool
assertions.reason: Response does not provide gcloud commands for configuring surge settings, only shows YAML configuration

assertions.expectation: Suggests upgrading spot pools before on-demand pools since they're lower risk
assertions.reason: Response suggests the opposite - upgrading on-demand instances first, then spot instance pools
